      "details": "Let T(u)=1/2+sqrt(u-u^2). From the hypothesis, f(x+a)=T(f(x)). For every real x, T(f(x))>=1/2, hence f(x+a) is always in [1/2,1]. For any y in [1/2,1], the verified identity 1/4-(y-y^2)=(1/2-y)^2 gives sqrt(1/4-(y-y^2))=|1/2-y|=y-1/2, so T(y)=1/2+(y-1/2)=y. Therefore f(x+2a)=T(f(x+a))=f(x+a).
